signature_wines.name: Giacomo Conterno Barolo Riserva Monfortino
signature_wines.tasting_notes: The summit of traditional Barolo, from the Cascina Francia and Arione vineyards in Serralunga, with six to eight years in large old barrel. Made only in great years. Source: rarewineco.com.
signature_wines.description: Giacomo Conterno's reserve Barolo and the benchmark of the traditional school. Released only in exceptional vintages after long cask ageing.

signature_wines.name: Bartolo Mascarello Barolo
signature_wines.tasting_notes: A single blended Barolo co-fermented from four crus (Cannubi, San Lorenzo, Rue, Rocche), expressing the house belief that the most complete Barolo comes from mutually compensating sites. Source: rarewineco.com.
signature_wines.description: The cult traditionalist Barolo of the village, a four-cru blend famous for Maria Teresa's hand-drawn labels.

signature_wines.name: Gaja Sori Tildin
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Smoky and elegantly austere: graphite, licorice, red currants and violets with firm, polished tannin. James Suckling 97 points, 2021 vintage. Source: wilsondaniels.com.
signature_wines.description: Gaja's most celebrated Barbaresco cru, a south-facing hill above Costa Russi.

signature_wines.name: Gaja Barbaresco
signature_wines.tasting_notes: A rich, round multi-cru Barbaresco with strawberry, red fruit and Nebbiolo's tarry depth. Jancis Robinson 17 points, 2020 vintage. Source: wilsondaniels.com.
signature_wines.description: The village Barbaresco that put the appellation on the global map.

signature_wines.name: Vietti Barolo Rocche di Castiglione
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Perfumed red cherry and rose with fine-grained tannin and a long, mineral finish, from the steep Rocche di Castiglione cru. Source: vietti.com.
signature_wines.description: Vietti's most aromatic single-cru Barolo from Castiglione Falletto.

signature_wines.name: Poderi Aldo Conterno Barolo Granbussia Riserva
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Blended from the oldest Bussia vines (Romirasco, Cicala, Colonnello), made only in superior years and aged at least a decade before release. Source: poderialdoconterno.com.
signature_wines.description: Aldo Conterno's flagship Monforte Riserva, a benchmark of the Bussia amphitheatre.

signature_wines.name: Massolino Barolo Vigna Rionda Riserva
signature_wines.tasting_notes: A powerful, structured traditional Barolo from one of Serralunga's greatest crus, released only after long ageing. Source: massolino.it.
signature_wines.description: Massolino's benchmark long-aged Riserva from the great Vigna Rionda cru.

signature_wines.name: Paolo Scavino Barolo Bric del Fiasc
signature_wines.tasting_notes: A polished, concentrated modern-classic Barolo from the Bricco del Fiasco cru in Castiglione Falletto, one of the first single-vineyard Barolos of the modern era. Source: stradadelbarolo.it.
signature_wines.description: Scavino's famed single-vineyard Barolo, a modern-classic of polish and concentration.

signature_wines.name: Braida Bricco dell'Uccellone
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Giacomo Bologna's barrique-aged Barbera (first 1982) that redefined the grape as a serious, age-worthy red. Source: braida.it.
signature_wines.description: The wine that elevated Barbera from everyday red to age-worthy benchmark.

signature_wines.name: G.D. Vajra Barolo Bricco delle Viole
signature_wines.tasting_notes: A fragrant, high-toned Barolo from the highest corner of the appellation in Vergne, built on altitude and freshness. Source: gdvajra.it.
signature_wines.description: Vajra's flagship Barolo from the high Bricco delle Viole vineyard.

signature_wines.name: La Scolca Gavi dei Gavi Etichetta Nera
signature_wines.tasting_notes: The Black Label that defined premium Gavi, an elegant, fresh 100% Cortese of intense aromatic complexity that ages remarkably well. Source: lascolca.net.
signature_wines.description: Piedmont's benchmark white, the wine that made Gavi a fine-wine name.

signature_wines.name: Bruno Rocca Barbaresco Rabaja
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Rosehip, violet and cherry with buttery notes, soft broad tannins and a long, sumptuous finish, from the prestigious Rabaja cru. Source: brunorocca.it.
signature_wines.description: Bruno Rocca's flagship from one of Barbaresco's most prized crus.
